title: Slow Simmered Vietnamese Beef Pho
description: A hearty and flavorful Vietnamese noodle soup made with slow-cooked beef and simmered broth.
instructions: In a large Dutch oven or pot, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium-high heat.
Add the beef brisket and sear until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the sliced onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the sliced ginger, star anise, and cinnamon stick, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the fish sauce, soy sauce, and sugar (if using), and stir to combine.
Add the beef broth and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2-3 hours, or until the beef is tender.
Remove the beef from the pot and slice it thinly against the grain.
Strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot.
Bring the broth to a boil and cook until reduced slightly, about 10 minutes.
Cook the noodles according to the package instructions.
To assemble the pho, place some noodles in a bowl, add sliced beef, and pour the hot broth over the top.
Serve immediately and garnish with herbs, if desired.
